title: TEA Evidence Bundle
description: Portable validation package for one signed TEA object. A TEA Evidence Bundle binds together the signed object identity, the digital signature, signing certificate material, timestamp evidence, transparency evidence, and optional validation material needed for long-term and offline verification.

bundleVersion.description: Version of the TEA Evidence Bundle format.

timestamps.description: Timestamp evidence objects for the signature. In TEA-native deployments, at least one timestamp is expected by policy. Timestamps MUST bind to the signature or other canonical signed representation according to TEA policy.

transparency.description: Transparency evidence entries. TEA supports multiple systems such as Rekor, Sigsum, and future SCITT-based systems. Each entry MUST bind to the same signed object used for TEA validation.

algorithm.description: Digest algorithm identifier, for example sha-256.

value.description: Digest value, typically encoded using base64url or another deployment-defined encoding.

objectType.description: Type of signed TEA object. Typical values include tea-collection, sbom-artifact, discovery-document, and dsse-attestation.

objectId.description: Optional implementation-specific identifier for the signed object.

mediaType.description: Optional media type of the signed object.

location.description: Optional URI from which the object may be retrieved.

format.description: Signature container or representation format, for example jws-detached, cms-detached, cose-sign1, dsse-envelope.

algorithm.description: Signature algorithm identifier, for example EdDSA.

value.description: Serialized signature value or signature object in the declared format.

signatureDigest.description: Optional digest of the signature object. This is especially useful when timestamps and transparency evidence bind to the signature rather than the raw signed object.

format.description: Certificate serialization format, typically x509-pem or x509-der.

certificate.description: Signing certificate used to validate the signature.

chain.description: Optional certificate chain. This is commonly used for WebPKI deployments and may also be included in TEA-native environments if helpful for portability.

fingerprint.description: Optional fingerprint of the signing certificate.

format.description: Timestamp format identifier, for example rfc3161.

messageImprint.description: Optional message imprint extracted from the timestamp token. For RFC 3161, this SHOULD correspond to the hash of the signature or canonical signed representation according to TEA policy.

tsaCertificateChain.description: Optional TSA certificate chain included for portability and offline validation.

type.description: What transparency evidence is bound to. TEA policy typically prefers binding to the signature or timestamped signature, not merely the raw artifact.

transparencyVerification.description: System-specific transparency verification material. This intentionally remains flexible so the same bundle format can support Rekor, Sigsum, and future SCITT receipts.

integratedTime.description: Integration time or equivalent log-recorded time if provided by the transparency system.

inclusionProof.description: System-specific inclusion proof or equivalent proof object.

signedTreeHead.description: Signed tree head or equivalent append-only checkpoint.

receipt.description: Receipt object or serialized receipt for systems such as SCITT.

logSignature.description: Optional log signature or signed statement used by the transparency system.

witnessSignatures.description: Optional witness signatures, relevant for systems such as Sigsum.

system.description: Transparency system identifier. Implementations should support at least one such system and be prepared for more than one.

evidenceType.description: System-specific evidence type, for example hashedrekord, dsse, log-entry, receipt.

format.description: Serialization format of the trust anchor or verification key.

value.description: Serialized trust anchor or verification key material.

system.description: Optional system identifier to which this trust anchor applies, for example rekor, sigsum, scitt, tsa.

certificateDigest.description: Optional digest of the signing certificate.

tsaTrustAnchors.description: Optional TSA trust anchors included for portability and offline validation.

transparencyVerificationKeys.description: Optional transparency-system verification keys or trust anchors.

notes.description: Optional implementation notes or preservation comments.

notes: logged to two independent transparency systems
